analysis.problem: Crumbling water infrastructure in cities is leading to potential water shortages and inefficiencies in water management.
analysis.root_cause: Aging infrastructure and insufficient investment in modernization are causing water systems to become unreliable and inefficient.
leverage_points: Local governments and municipal authorities responsible for infrastructure maintenance
Private sector investment in smart water technologies

proposed_actions.action: Implement smart water metering systems across all urban areas
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Reduce water wastage by 30% within five years through real-time monitoring and leak detection

proposed_actions.action: Launch a public-private partnership initiative to fund infrastructure upgrades
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Secure 50% of funding for water system modernization from private investors within three years

raw_items.title: Smart cities
raw_items.summary: A smart city is an urban model that leverages technology, human capital, and governance to improve sustainability, efficiency, and social inclusion, which are considered goals for cities of the future.
